Course Title: AI in Hospital Operations and Scheduling
Category: Healthcare & Life Sciences: Medical Services, Pharmacy, Biotechnology, Hospitals
Who is this for: Hospital administrators, nursing managers, clinical supervisors, operations managers, department heads, medical practice managers, healthcare quality officers, patient safety coordinators
Team or Dept this is for: Hospital Operations, Clinical Operations, Nursing, Quality Assurance, Patient Safety, Healthcare Administration, Medical Affairs
Description: When optimisation creates unintended patient care issues.
Prerequisites: A basic familiarity with hospital or clinical workflows, along with a general curiosity about how technology is used in healthcare settings, is all that is needed to get started with this course.
Learning objectives: By the end of this course, you will be able to identify common ways that AI-driven scheduling and optimisation tools can create unintended patient care issues within hospital operations, and explain the key factors that contribute to these outcomes. You will also apply practical strategies to recognise early warning signs of AI-related disruptions and take informed steps to prioritise patient safety when evaluating or working alongside automated hospital systems.
